real_insight: The on-call engineer's response procedure should remain identical whether the dashboard is rising or falling — i.e., metric-reading should not become self-reading; the coupling between operator-state and system-state is the variable to break.
objection: The phrase 'the operator-state did not move' is on the line between specific engineering observation and aphorism. It survives the line because the analog ties it to a measurable behavioral consequence (under load, the self-coupled engineer 'will defend instead of debug'; under success, 'will overcommit instead of measure'). 'Defend instead of debug' is a real, named pattern any senior engineer recognizes from incident reviews — it is the exact failure mode of post-incident blamelessness violations. 'Equanimity is the precondition for accurate perception under load' is the kind of sentence I would normally flag as cope, but the analog earns it by naming the falsification (stoic suppression ≠ samatvam) and the operational artifact (procedure invariance across green/red dashboard states). The PagerDuty / Datadog / Postgres / runbook / Sentry stack-naming is concrete. The 'defend instead of debug' axis would survive the question 'would a senior engineer find this new or correctly named?' — yes, the operator-system-coupling label is a precise name for a pattern most engineers feel but do not articulate. Pass.
